response.justification: Bogdan is a highly experienced software craftsman with strong web and TypeScript foundations, but the role demands deep C++ expertise as its core requirement — his C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor-thesis project. There is no evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443), industrial protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or embedded/IoT domain depth, all of which are central to this position. His strengths in agile practices and high-throughput systems are positive signals but insufficient to offset the fundamental skills mismatch.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and pairing across multiple roles
Demonstrated ability to build and operate high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic)
Relevant industrial tooling exposure at National Instruments writing C/C++ components for LabWindows/CVI, showing baseline familiarity with engineering software
concerns: C++ is not a current or primary skill — the resume's skills list omits it entirely and the only substantive C++ work is 10+ years old
No industrial cybersecurity background: no mention of IEC 62443, SDL practices, or security-focused engineering in any role
No exposure to industrial or IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850) that are critical for this automation-domain position
